qwen2.5:7b

You're running an unsigned public beta of The Civic Desk. Windows SmartScreen may warn on install; that's expected. Report issues on GitHub.

MORNING EDITOR PACKET

Daily Scan

Run deterministic record checks first, then use local AI only to summarize and rank what needs an editor's look.

Refresh
Run Daily Scan
SOURCES WATCHED
6
OPEN LEADS
15
DRAFTS IN DESK
5
AI STATUS
Ready

AI BRIEF

Latest scan #2

Civic Desk keeps this pass local, private, and evidence-first. Use it to find the handful of records worth turning into leads. If local AI is unavailable, deterministic checks still build a review packet.

Scan Results for Scan #2

CITY CLERK'S OFFICE / PUBLIC NOTICE

City Clerk’s Agenda Management Portal Issues
High priority

The Building Services online permitting portal of the City of Longmont is currently experiencing technical issues, affecting residents and businesses seeking permits.

Why this was flagged

Technical problems with essential city services impact public convenience and can lead to administrative delays for permit seekers.

Suggested next step

Report the issue to the relevant department and follow up on potential solutions.

Open source and review
Aggregated across sources

CITY WEBSITE / OFFICIAL UPDATE

Youth Center Programs in Longmont
Medium priority

The City of Longmont is focusing on youth development through programs at the Youth Center to support children, families, and communities.

Why this was flagged

These programs can impact local youth well-being and community development efforts.

Suggested next step

Research the specific initiatives and their potential impacts on the youth population.

No original source URL
Aggregated across sources

CITY OF LONGMONT / PUBLIC NOTICE

City Council Second Reading Process
High priority

The City Council's process for approving ordinances, including the steps of first and second readings, public hearings, and voting requirements, is outlined. This transparency helps ensure the public understands how local laws are enacted.

Why this was flagged

This information provides insight into the legislative process for city ordinances, which affects residents' daily lives and is crucial for understanding local governance.

Suggested next step

Review the detailed steps to ensure clarity and accuracy before publication.

Open source and review
Aggregated across sources

CITY OF LONGMONT PUBLIC INFORMATION DEPARTMENT / COMMUNITY SIGNAL

Longmont Invites Applications for New Official City Flag
High priority

The city of Longmont is seeking public input to design its new official flag, engaging community members in the process of creating a symbol that represents their diverse and welcoming community.

Why this was flagged

This project involves the community in a meaningful way by allowing residents to contribute to the city's visual identity, which can foster pride and civic engagement.

Suggested next step

Contact the public information office for more details on how residents can submit designs.

Open source and review
Aggregated across sources
